title: Behen vulgaris
intro:
Borago officinalis, not Behen vulgaris, is a plant I couldn't find information on. Behen is a common name for the plant Borago officinalis is not, so I'm going to give you information on the plant Linum usitatissimum and then the plant Borago officinalis. 

Here's a 4-sentence paragraph about Linum usitatissimum (Flaxseed):
Linum usitatissimum, commonly known as flax or flaxseed, is an annual plant in the family Linaceae. The health benefits of flaxseed include its high content of omega-3 fatty acids and dietary fiber, which may help lower cholesterol levels and regulate blood sugar levels. The bioactive constituents of flaxseed include alpha-linolenic acid (ALA), linoleic acid, and lignans, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The most common herbal preparations made with flaxseed include flaxseed oil, flaxseed capsules, and ground flaxseed, which can be added to food or taken as a supplement.

Here's a 4-sentence paragraph about Borago officinalis (Borage):
Borago officinalis, commonly known as borage, is an annual herb in the family Boraginaceae. The health benefits of borage include its high content of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), which may help reduce inflammation and improve skin health. The bioactive constituents of borage include GLA, vitamin C, and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The most common herbal preparations made with borage include borage oil, borage tea, and borage supplements, which can be used to promote skin health and reduce inflammation.

side_effects: Behen vulgaris, also known as flaxseed, is generally considered safe for consumption in moderate amounts. However, excessive consumption of flaxseed may cause gastrointestinal side effects such as bloating, gas, and diarrhea.
